private.name: NODA AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $125M

data.narrative:
Sagar Defence Engineering is a Series A-stage defense and security company that has raised $25.4M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparison universe of 10 peers—2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms in the defense and security sector at Series A/B stages, including Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Code Metal, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The Stage-Progression method uses the $25M raised as a proxy for current valuation and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage progression, implying a valuation of $51M. The Peer-Median method anchors to the median valuation of the 8 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables, which stands at $1.0B, with a range spanning $125M (minimum) to $3.0B (maximum).

The triangulated analysis yields a low estimate of $43M, a median of $525M, and a high of $1.1B. The wide dispersion between methods reflects the heterogeneity inherent in frontier-defense valuations at this stage. The Stage-Progression method produces a conservative floor based on capital deployment, while the Peer-Median approach reflects market pricing for more mature defense-tech entrants. Key uncertainties include the company's revenue scale relative to the peer set, the timing and certainty of government procurement contracts, and whether peer valuations reflect sustainable unit economics or growth-stage premiums that may not apply uniformly across the sector.
